title: Performance Audit Output
description: Output of PerfAudit agent node (commercial-cert only, R9 NEW). Runs Lighthouse CI / bundle analyzer / k6-light. Consumed by deterministic PerfGate op.

_canonical_threshold_policy._doc: DOUBLE-WRITE anchor (slice qa-a11y-perf, 2026-06-05). Canonical Core Web Vitals gate floors that MUST be mirrored into ctx.policy.perf_floor when enterprise-qa-testing launches the commercial-cert workflow. Field names match exactly what qa-orchestrator.js `perf_gate_policy` reads (floor.max_lcp_ms / max_inp_ms / max_cls / max_tbt_ms / max_bundle_size_bytes). Baked default ALSO lives in presets/commercial-cert.json context.policy.perf_floor. CRITICAL: qa-orchestrator.js perf_gate_policy defaults any UNSET floor field to Infinity (never blocks) — so leaving perf_floor unset = silent non-enforcement. Authoring this here + baking the preset default closes that hole. ADDITIVE / non-enforced JSON-Schema field (descriptive only).
_canonical_threshold_policy._cwv_note: Thresholds are the Google Core Web Vitals 'good' boundaries. INP (Interaction to Next Paint) REPLACED FID (First Input Delay) as a Core Web Vital on 2024-03-12; there is no FID field in this schema.
_canonical_threshold_policy._decision_semantics: lcp_ms or inp_ms over threshold => BLOCK (CWV core); cls/tbt/bundle over threshold => WARN. max_bundle_size_bytes is intentionally NOT baked globally (route/app dependent — set per project in ctx.policy.perf_floor.max_bundle_size_bytes; unset => not gated).

decision_hint.description: Agent's draft assessment; deterministic PerfGate makes final decision
